title: Archive: Fracture / Soft-Wrist Assembly
description: Archive reason: ShapeForce now covers the low-cost soft robotic wrist for contact-rich manipulation direction directly, while fractured-object reassembly remains too far from current hardware/data priorities to justify a combined Survey card.
summary: The combined Fracture / Soft-Wrist Assembly survey is archived. ShapeForce provides the concrete low-cost, plug-and-play soft-wrist contact-feedback system and contact-rich manipulation evaluation that the soft-wrist half of the card needed; the remaining fractured-object reassembly half should only reopen as a separate benchmark if there is a physical object set, simulator/real protocol, and owner.

details.text: ShapeForce directly addresses the soft-wrist project: it proposes a low-cost, plug-and-play compliant wrist that turns external force/torque into marker-tracked deformation and force-like signals, then evaluates it across contact-rich manipulation tasks against six-axis force-torque sensing. This completes the standalone soft-wrist/contact-feedback angle for our dashboard.

Earlier soft-wrist insertion work remains useful background for sim-to-real contact-rich insertion, but it is no longer enough to keep this as an open Survey project after ShapeForce covers the cheaper hardware and policy-evaluation route.

The fractured-object reassembly half is not being claimed as solved by ShapeForce. It is archived because it should not be bundled with soft-wrist hardware anymore; reopen it only as a narrower fracture/reassembly benchmark with real object assets, sequencing/planning metrics, and a clear relation to current robot infrastructure.

details: Moved-out live work: VTLA should keep tactile/contact representation questions; Real Robot Infra should handle any actual wrist/force-sensor hardware purchase or build; UMI/World-Model work should only borrow contact-rich task cases if they become part of a real data-collection plan.
risks_decisions: Archived 2026-06-05 after user flagged ShapeForce as completing the soft-wrist direction.
Reason type: paper completed the core low-cost soft-wrist contact-rich manipulation topic; remaining value is background or a hardware decision, not an independent Survey project.

Fractured-object reassembly is archived with this mixed card only because it lacks a current owner, object set, simulator/real split, and acceptance metrics. It can be reopened as its own project if those become concrete.
Reopen condition: we commit to a specific assembly benchmark with real parts/fixtures, policy inputs, success metrics, and a named owner.
